media_attachments.description:
De samenwerking tussen universiteiten en Defensie krijgt in rap tempo een structureel karakter. Naast de militaire minors schieten op verschillende plekken, soms op de universiteitscampus zelf, ‘veiligheidscampussen’, ‘ecosystemen’ of ‘innovatiehubs’ uit de grond, waarin Defensie en universiteit samen optrekken. Koepelorganisatie Universiteiten van Nederland werkt met het ministerie aan een landelijke raamwerkovereenkomst voor onderzoek.

Twee financiële ontwikkelingen lopen daarin parallel. Het kabinet-Schoof kondigde historische miljardenbezuinigingen op het onderwijs aan, die door het kabinet-Jetten slechts deels zijn teruggedraaid. Tegelijk stijgen de defensie-uitgaven van zo’n 20 naar bijna 27 miljard euro in twee jaar. Defensie en de universiteit treffen elkaar dus in een ongelijke machtsverhouding, wat voor academici een vraag over de eigen identiteit oproept: wat voor universiteit willen wij hebben, een dienstbare of een kritische?

card.description:
Важливо, що активізована робота з партнерами для синхронізації санкцій – українських у юрисдикціях партнерів і партнерських у нашій юрисдикції. Міністр закордонних справ Андрій Сибіга доповів щодо таких та інших контактів. Зокрема, більш системно працюємо по тіньовому зерновому флоту Росії – по суднах, які використовуються для перевезення зерна, що було вкрадене на тимчасово окупованій території України. 

Цими днями був гострий ізраїльський трек по такому вкраденому зерну, і наші дипломати та Офіс Генерального прокурора включились у відповідну роботу. Будуємо систему протидії тіньовому зерновому флоту, так само як і тіньовому нафтовому флоту. 

Також обговорили з міністром пріоритети на європейському напрямку: Україні потрібні хороші дипломатичні результати травня. Погодили й вирішення деяких кадрових питань у структурі МЗС України та в нашому посольському корпусі. Визначили й позиції щодо голосувань у міжнародних організаціях в інтересах миру та України. Слава Україні!

card.title: maxine 🇵🇸 (@maxine@hachyderm.io)
card.description:
Open source continues to struggle because above all else it has persisted in the idea of corporate dependence. We moved to Sourceforge and it was a mess. We moved to Github which gave us even less individual control and it also became a mess. In between was also Launchpad but tbh that never really took off bc of bzr.

The alternative isn’t self-hosting though. Large projects provide code hosting for anything vaguely relevant. Umbrella hosting and cooperating on these services makes things more sustainable and makes anything less likely to disappear.

Over a decade ago I used to lend my time to various places to assist with hosting or just initial set up, and then everything started disappearing, wikis got swallowed by fandom and ign, every major project moved to github, and everyone else pretended you need a k8s cluster to run anything. We could just bring that back. We even learned a lot of important lessons from the likes of Freenode about how to avoid hostile takeovers.

content:
Yikes, forget about Linux security

Copy Fail: 732 Bytes to Root on Every Major #Linux Distribution

“A single 732-byte #Python script can edit a setuid binary and obtain root on essentially all Linux distributions shipped since 2017.

The kernel never marks the corrupted page dirty for writeback, so the file on disk remains unchanged and ordinary on-disk checksum comparisons miss the modification.”

https://bit.ly/4w1TWu3 #ubuntu

media_attachments.description:
Text Shot: This finding was AI-assisted, but began with an insight from Theori researcher Taeyang Lee, who was studying how the Linux crypto subsystem interacts with page-cache-backed data. He used Xint Code to scale his research across the entire crypto subsystem, and Copy Fail was the most critical finding in the report.

This is the first in a two-part series:

Part 1 (this post): The bug and local privilege escalation
Part 2: Kubernetes container escape
What Makes Copy Fail Different

The Linux kernel has had high-profile privilege escalation bugs before. Dirty Cow (CVE-2016-5195) required winning a race condition in the VM subsystem's copy-on-write path. It often needed multiple attempts and sometimes crashed the system. Dirty Pipe (CVE-2022-0847) was version-specific and required precise pipe buffer manipulation.

Copy Fail is a straight-line logic flaw. It triggers without races, retries, or crash-prone timing windows.

content:
Sicilianews24.it: Escursionista aggredita da cinque cani a Monte Gallo: salvata in elicottero

Paura nel primo pomeriggio di ieri a Monte Gallo, dove una escursionista svizzera di 51 anni è stata aggredita da cinque cani di grossa taglia mentre si trovava nella zona sommitale del rilievo.
L’allarme è scattato intorno alle 14.00, quando la Centrale Operativa 118 Palermo-Trapani ha attivato la stazione locale del Corpo Nazionale Soccorso Alpino e Speleologico per un intervento d’urgenza. Le condizioni della donna sono apparse subito gravi: presentava profonde ferite da morso su diverse parti del corpo e una significativa emorragia.
Due squadre di tecnici del Soccorso Alpino sono partite rapidamente da Palermo, mentre veniva richiesto anche il supporto aereo dell’Aeronautica Militare. In particolare, è intervenuto un elicottero dell’82° Centro SAR del 15° Stormo, decollato dalla base di Trapani-Birgi, per garantire un recupero tempestivo.

La donna trasportata in ospedale in eliccottero
Durante le operazioni, il velivolo ha imbarcato un tecnico di elisoccorso presso il 4° Reparto Volo della Polizia di Stato, per poi dirigersi verso la vetta di Monte Gallo. La donna è stata raggiunta contemporaneamente dalle squadre a terra e dall’elicottero. Dopo una prima valutazione sanitaria sul posto, è stata recuperata mediante verricello e trasportata in sicurezza.
L’elisoccorso ha quindi trasferito la ferita presso l’aeroporto di Boccadifalco, dove ad attenderla c’era un’ambulanza del 118. Da lì è stata condotta all’Ospedale Ingrassia per le cure necessarie.
Fondamentale la sinergia tra i diversi enti coinvolti, tra cui la Centrale Operativa 118 Palermo-Trapani e il 4° Reparto Volo della Polizia di Stato, che hanno collaborato per portare a termine con successo un intervento complesso e delicato.
Resta ora da chiarire la provenienza dei cani e le circostanze che hanno portato all’aggressione.
L'articolo Escursionista aggredita da cinque cani a Monte Gallo: salvata in elicottero sembra essere il primo su Sicilianews24.it.

Hiker attacked by five dogs in Monte Gallo: rescued by helicopter

Fear gripped the early afternoon yesterday in Monte Gallo, where a 51-year-old Swiss hiker was attacked by five large dogs while she was in the summit area of the ridge.

The alarm was raised around 2 p.m., when the 118 Palermo-Trapani Operational Center activated the local station of the National Alpine and Speleological Rescue Corps for an urgent intervention. The woman’s condition appeared immediately serious: she presented deep bite wounds on various parts of her body and significant bleeding.

Two teams of Alpine Rescue technicians quickly departed from Palermo, while support from the Italian Air Force was also requested. Specifically, an helicopter from the 82nd SAR Center of the 15th Stormo, took off from Trapani-Birgi base, to ensure a timely recovery.

The woman transported by helicopter

During the operations, the aircraft embarked an air rescue technician at the 4th Flight Squadron of the Italian Police, before heading towards the summit of Monte Gallo. The woman was reached simultaneously by the ground teams and the helicopter. After a preliminary health assessment on site, she was retrieved using a winch and safely transported.

The air rescue then transferred the injured woman to the Boccadifalco airport, where an 118 ambulance was waiting. From there, she was taken to the Ingrassia Hospital for necessary treatment.

Crucial was the synergy between the various entities involved, including the 118 Palermo-Trapani Operational Center and the 4th Flight Squadron of the Italian Police, which collaborated to successfully complete a complex and delicate intervention.

The origin of the dogs and the circumstances that led to the attack now need to be clarified.

The article Swiss Hiker Attacked by Five Dogs in Monte Gallo: Saved by Helicopter appears first on Sicilianews24.it.
